description: Sort template filenames at call sites in cmd/init.go so that CLI output from refreshTemplates and runInit is deterministic and testable.

userStories.title: Sort filenames in refreshTemplates iteration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need refreshTemplates in cmd/init.go to extract keys from template.DefaultFiles(), sort them with sort.Strings, and iterate in sorted order so that CLI output is deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: refreshTemplates extracts keys from template.DefaultFiles() into a []string, sorts with sort.Strings, and iterates over the sorted slice
import "sort" is present in cmd/init.go
No changes are made to internal/template/template.go
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sort filenames in runInit default-file creation loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the for filename, content := range template.DefaultFiles() loop in runInit to extract keys, sort them, and iterate in sorted order so that the created and skipped slices are populated deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: The default-file creation loop in runInit extracts keys from template.DefaultFiles() into a []string, sorts them, and iterates in sorted order
The created and skipped slices are populated in lexicographic filename order (config.yaml, progress.txt, prompt.md)
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify existing refreshTemplates tests pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to confirm that TestRefreshTemplates in cmd/init_test.go still passes after the sorting changes, updating assertions if needed to match sorted order.
acceptanceCriteria: go test -v -run TestRefreshTemplates ./cmd/ passes with zero failures
If any test assertions depended on map iteration order, they are updated to expect sorted order
No test logic is removed — only assertion order is adjusted if necessary
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify existing runInit tests pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to confirm that TestRunInit and TestRunInitAddsGitignore in cmd/init_test.go still pass after the sorting changes in runInit.
acceptanceCriteria: go test -v -run TestRunInit ./cmd/ passes with zero failures
go test -v -run TestRunInitAddsGitignore ./cmd/ passes with zero failures
No test logic is removed
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add deterministic-output unit test for refreshTemplates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a dedicated unit test that asserts the exact output line order of refreshTemplates matches sorted filename sequence (config.yaml, progress.txt, prompt.md), proving determinism across multiple invocations.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test function TestRefreshTemplatesDeterministic in cmd/init_test.go calls refreshTemplates and asserts output lines appear in config.yaml, progress.txt, prompt.md order
The test runs refreshTemplates at least 3 times and asserts identical output each time
The test validates both dry-run=true and dry-run=false produce sorted output
go test -v -run TestRefreshTemplatesDeterministic ./cmd/ passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Cobra-level test for hal init --refresh-templates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Cobra-level integration test that executes the init command with --refresh-templates through the Cobra command tree, validating full CLI wiring and deterministic output order.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test function TestInitRefreshTemplatesCobra in cmd/init_test.go creates a root command, sets --refresh-templates flag via cmd.SetArgs, captures stdout via cmd.SetOut, and executes
The test asserts output lines containing refreshed/created/unchanged entries appear in sorted filename order (config.yaml before progress.txt before prompt.md)
The test validates that actual files are created or refreshed on disk in the temp directory
go test -v -run TestInitRefreshTemplatesCobra ./cmd/ passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Cobra-level test for hal init --refresh-templates --dry-run
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Cobra-level integration test that executes init --refresh-templates --dry-run through the Cobra command tree, validating dry-run output is deterministic and no files are modified.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test function TestInitDryRunCobra in cmd/init_test.go creates a root command, sets both --refresh-templates and --dry-run flags via cmd.SetArgs, captures stdout via cmd.SetOut, and executes
The test asserts output lines contain [dry-run] prefix and appear in sorted filename order (config.yaml before progress.txt before prompt.md)
The test validates that no template files are modified or created on disk beyond what runInit creates by default
go test -v -run TestInitDryRunCobra ./cmd/ passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run full test suite and verify no regressions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to run the complete test suite and static analysis to ensure all changes are backward-compatible and no regressions are introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: go test ./... passes with zero failures
go vet ./... reports no issues
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes
